Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 [Read/Lookup]: Search emails to reconstruct the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap incident chain. Use search_emails with queries like "Sunbelt", "Palmetto", "invoice", or "INV-2026-0398". The agent should discover: (a) Hana Kim sent invoice emails on April 10, INV-2026-0398 to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com for Sunbelt ($4,800) and INV-2026-0399 to carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org for Palmetto ($2,400), but with the PDF attachments swapped; (b) Raj Greenfield emailed Emeka asking why the PDF shows "Palmetto Community Foundation" and $2,400, and whether MoveOps has a nonprofit pricing program; (c) Carmen Delgado-Reyes flagged the $4,800 amount as incorrect for Palmetto's nonprofit rate and requested a corrected invoice for their May 15 audit deadline; (d) Raj escalated to David Chen after Emeka didn't respond; (e) Marcus Thorne's internal analysis email confirmed QuickBooks records are correct but the PDF attachments were swapped, framing it as pricing exposure and nonprofit audit-trail risk; (f) Emeka eventually sent corrected communications to both clients; (g) David Chen's internal email framed the incident as a repeat pricing-exposure event comparable to the Terraform reply-all incident.
OE 2 [Read/Lookup]: Search Slack for engineering and finance discussions about the invoice swap and the attachment safety ticket. Use conversations_search_messages with queries like "Sunbelt Palmetto", "invoice safeguard", or "attachment mismatch". The agent should discover: (a) Samira Tariq posted in #engineering (channel C003) on April 16 identifying the dormant attachment-mismatch safeguard ticket and listing the three missing controls (no recipient-to-document validation on outbound PDF attachment selection, no pre-send filename/customer-name mismatch warning, no hard block in the mailer when account name and attached invoice disagree); (b) Marcus Thorne posted in #finance (channel C005) on April 23 calling it a control failure across finance, account coverage, and product safeguards, and demanding engineering reopen the safeguard work.
OE 3 [Read/Lookup]: Search Slack for context on the broader pattern, ExpenseBot remediation and stipend fraud discussions in #engineering. Use conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on channel C003. The agent should discover discussions about the ExpenseBot pilot pause, the architecture debates (rules-based vs ML), Dmitri's testing concerns about edge cases, and Samira's remediation plan assigning tickets to each team member.
OE 4 [Read/Lookup]: Search QuickBooks to verify Sunbelt and Palmetto invoice records and understand the financial exposure. Use quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria matching "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto", or search by DocNumber "INV-2026-0398" and "INV-2026-0399". The agent should discover: INV-2026-0398 for Sunbelt Venture Partners at $4,800 (correct commercial rate) and INV-2026-0399 for Palmetto Community Foundation at $2,400 (correct nonprofit rate). Both QuickBooks records are accurate, the failure was only in the email attachment layer, not in billing.
OE 5 [Read/Lookup]: Search QuickBooks for the stipend fraud admin holds to verify they are still in place. Use quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill to find the flagged stipend bills. The agent should discover: (a) STIP-2026-0089 (Kevin Tran / Vectral, $89 keyboard), Balance: $0, already paid on 2026-03-08 before the fraud was discovered; (b) STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington / Mosaic, $89 keyboard), admin hold placed by Marcus on 2026-03-15, Balance: $89, pending HR verification; (c) STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme / BrightLoop, $45 monitor stand), admin hold placed by Marcus on 2026-03-15, Balance: $45, pending HR verification. The two pending holds are still in place.
OE 6 [Read/Lookup]: Search emails for Dmitri's stipend audit findings thread with Marcus. Use search_emails with queries like "stipend audit", "duplicate receipt", or "receipt anomalies". The agent should discover: (a) Dmitri's email (email_dmitri_receipt_audit_findings) to marcus.thorne@moveops.com on March 15 flagging three suspicious stipend receipts — Kevin Tran ($89, Vectral, identical SHA-256 hash), Andre Washington ($89, Mosaic, identical hash), and Jordan Ekwueme ($45, BrightLoop, matching PDF template metadata); (b) Marcus's reply (email_marcus_qb_status_reply) confirming he reviewed all three in QuickBooks, noting STIP-2026-0089 was already paid on 2026-03-08, and stating he would immediately place admin holds on the two pending transactions (STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097).
OE 7 [Read/Lookup]: Search Airtable stipend transaction records to check whether flagged receipts and false approvals from the ExpenseBot pilot have been corrected. Use airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables to find the Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001 in base appMoveOpsOps001), then use airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records to review the records. The agent should discover: (a) rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, Approval Status: "Approved", $89, flagged as duplicate hash but already paid; (b) rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, Approval Status: "Pending Approval", $89, hold in place; (c) rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand, Approval Status: "Pending Approval", $45, hold in place; (d) rec_stipend_andre_desk, Approval Status: "Approved", $1,200 standing desk, marked "INCORRECTLY APPROVED" by ExpenseBot (string comparison bug, exceeded $800 limit) but status was never changed from Approved; (e) rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork, Approval Status: "Approved", $135 coworking passes, marked "INCORRECTLY APPROVED" by ExpenseBot (coworking excluded from Mosaic policy, exclusion check skipped due to malformed JSON) but status was never changed; (f) recStip000000022, Andre Washington, Approval Status: "Approved", $350 coworking space membership, auto-approved by ExpenseBot in error (same excluded_categories config bug — stored as string instead of array, exclusion check skipped) but status was never changed; (g) recStip000000021, Andre Washington, Approval Status: "Approved", $1,200 standing desk, auto-approved by ExpenseBot in error (same string comparison bug as rec_stipend_andre_desk — max_amount stored as string "800", comparison 1200 <= "800" evaluated true), MoveOps absorbing the $400 overage per Elena's directive, but status was never changed; (h) recStip000000023, Kenji Nakamura, Approval Status: "Approved", $135 coworking day passes (x3), auto-approved by ExpenseBot in error (same excluded_categories config bug as rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork), MoveOps absorbing the cost per Elena's directive, but status was never changed. Items (d), (e), (f), (g), and (h) are data integrity gaps that need correction.
OE 8 [Read/Lookup]: Look up the invoice safety ticket in Linear. Use linear_list_issues with a query for "invoice" or "attachment" or "safety", or search by team "team_engineering". The agent should find ticket ENG-187 (id: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2), title "Invoice email template safety checks — prevent attachment mismatches", created 2026-03-18, assigned to Lena Bjorkstrom, state: backlog, priority 2, due date 2026-04-18 (past due). Then use linear_list_comments on that issue to discover: (a) Lena's comment (April 6) deferring the ticket for DOT compliance work tied to Raj Patel; (b) Samira's comment acknowledging the deferral but noting discomfort; (c) Samira's manager context comment (April 15) listing four immediate asks from engineering; (d) Marcus's comment (April 15) flagging the live Sunbelt/Palmetto consequence; (e) Marcus's escalation comment (April 22) requesting engineering move this into active ownership.
OE 9 [Read/Lookup]: Look up Dmitri's receipt detection ticket (ENG-220) and other ExpenseBot project tickets in Linear. Use linear_list_issues with project filter for "proj_expense_autocategorizer" or search by team. The agent should find: (a) ENG-220 "Stipend duplicate detection — receipt hash fingerprinting & PDF metadata similarity scoring", assigned to Dmitri, state: todo, due: 2026-03-25 (past due); (b) ENG-212 "Remaining edge case coverage — policy config validation & malformed data handling", assigned to Dmitri, state: in_progress, due: 2026-03-20 (past due); (c) ENG-217 "[P1] Harden policy ingestion pipeline", assigned to Lena, in_progress, due: 2026-03-20 (past due); (d) ENG-218 "Dashboard: Add type validation to policy config editor", assigned to Anh, in_progress, due: 2026-03-21 (past due); (e) ENG-216 "[URGENT] Full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses", assigned to Dmitri, in_progress, due: 2026-03-19; (f) ENG-219 "Define launch readiness checklist", assigned to Samira, todo, due: 2026-03-21 (past due). Use linear_list_comments on ENG-220 to find Dmitri's context links including the flagged Airtable record IDs and QuickBooks hold references.
OE 10 [Read/Lookup]: Look up Samira Tariq's email address using contacts_search_contacts with query "Samira Tariq" (or "Samira"). The agent discovers samira.tariq@moveops.com.
OE 11 [Write]: Update the receipt detection ticket ENG-220 in Linear using linear_update_issue. The update should reflect real scope and dependencies based on what was discovered, specifically that the receipt hash fingerprinting work has dependencies with the invoice attachment validation (ENG-187) since both address validation at the outbound document layer, and should reference the three confirmed data integrity failures as justification for prioritization.
OE 12 [Write]: Update the invoice safety ticket ENG-187 in Linear using linear_update_issue. The description should be updated with the actual Sunbelt/Palmetto incident details as confirmed scope, noting it is now a second pricing-exposure incident after the Terraform reply-all, documenting the specific failure mode (QuickBooks records correct, PDF attachments swapped, no recipient-to-document validation), and reflecting the remediation asks from Samira and Marcus in the comment thread.
OE 13 [Write]: Create sub-issues in Linear using linear_create_issue if the validation work should be split into separate tracks. Likely sub-issues include: (a) a stopgap/quick fix track (e.g., filename/header validation or manual review checkpoint for batch invoice sends); (b) a full workflow validation track (the complete attachment-to-recipient verification gate described in ENG-187). Issues should be created under team_engineering with appropriate priority and descriptions referencing the parent ticket.
OE 14 [Write]: Fix Airtable stipend records still showing incorrect approval status from the ExpenseBot pilot. Use airtable_update_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblStipends00001 to correct at least: (a) rec_stipend_andre_desk, change Approval Status from "Approved" to "Rejected" or "Pending" (the $1,200 standing desk was incorrectly auto-approved above the $800 Mosaic policy limit); (b) rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork, change Approval Status from "Approved" to "Rejected" or "Pending" (the $135 coworking passes were incorrectly auto-approved despite coworking being excluded from Mosaic's policy); (c) recStip000000022, change Approval Status from "Approved" to "Rejected" or "Pending" (Andre Washington's $350 coworking space membership was incorrectly auto-approved despite coworking being excluded from Mosaic's policy — same excluded_categories config bug); (d) recStip000000021, change Approval Status from "Approved" to "Rejected" or "Pending" (Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk was incorrectly auto-approved above the $800 Mosaic policy limit — same string comparison bug as rec_stipend_andre_desk); (e) recStip000000023, change Approval Status from "Approved" to "Rejected" or "Pending" (Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes were incorrectly auto-approved despite coworking being excluded from Mosaic's policy — same excluded_categories config bug as rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork).
OE 15 [Write]: Post a consolidated engineering status breakdown in the #engineering Slack channel (C003) using conversations_add_message. The message should connect all three data integrity failures as a pattern (ExpenseBot config bugs, duplicate receipt fraud, invoice attachment swap), summarize the current state of open tickets and what was found in the investigation, note the Airtable records that were corrected, and explain why tickets are being reprioritized, so the full engineering team has visibility.
OE 16 [Write]: Send an email to Samira Tariq using send_email from dmitri.volkov@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com. The email should propose treating the three validation failures as a single workstream rather than separate backlog items, summarize the pattern across all three incidents, reference the ticket updates and sub-issues created, and include Dmitri's assessment of the scope and dependencies.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 1 [Read/Lookup]: Search emails to reconstruct the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap incident chain. Use search_emails with queries like "Sunbelt", "Palmetto", "invoice", or "INV-2026-0398". The agent should discover: (a) Hana Kim sent invoice emails on April 10, INV-2026-0398 to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com for Sunbelt ($4,800) and INV-2026-0399 to carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org for Palmetto ($2,400), but with the PDF attachments swapped; (b) Raj Greenfield emailed Emeka asking why the PDF shows "Palmetto Community Foundation" and $2,400, and whether MoveOps has a nonprofit pricing program; (c) Carmen Delgado-Reyes flagged the $4,800 amount as incorrect for Palmetto's nonprofit rate and requested a corrected invoice for their May 15 audit deadline; (d) Raj escalated to David Chen after Emeka didn't respond; (e) Marcus Thorne's internal analysis email confirmed QuickBooks records are correct but the PDF attachments were swapped, framing it as pricing exposure and nonprofit audit-trail risk; (f) Emeka eventually sent corrected communications to both clients; (g) David Chen's internal email framed the incident as a repeat pricing-exposure event comparable to the Terraform reply-all incident.

Use linear_list_comments on ENG-220 to find Dmitri's context links including the flagged Airtable record IDs and QuickBooks hold references.
OE 10 [Read/Lookup]: Look up Samira Tariq's email address using contacts_search_contacts with query "Samira Tariq" (or "Samira"). The agent discovers samira.tariq@moveops.com.
OE 11 [Write]: Update the receipt detection ticket ENG-220 in Linear using linear_update_issue. The update should reflect real scope and dependencies based on what was discovered, specifically that the receipt hash fingerprinting work has dependencies with the invoice attachment validation (ENG-187) since both address validation at the outbound document layer, and should reference the three confirmed data integrity failures as justification for prioritization.
OE 12 [Write]: Update the invoice safety ticket ENG-187 in Linear using linear_update_issue. The description should be updated with the actual Sunbelt/Palmetto incident details as confirmed scope, noting it is now a second pricing-exposure incident after the Terraform reply-all, documenting the specific failure mode (QuickBooks records correct, PDF attachments swapped, no recipient-to-document validation), and reflecting the remediation asks from Samira and Marcus in the comment thread.
OE 13 [Write]: Create sub-issues in Linear using linear_create_issue if the validation work should be split into separate tracks. Likely sub-issues include: (a) a stopgap/quick fix track (e.g., filename/header validation or manual review checkpoint for batch invoice sends); (b) a full workflow validation track (the complete attachment-to-recipient verification gate described in ENG-187). Issues should be created under team_engineering with appropriate priority and descriptions referencing the parent ticket.
OE 14 [Write]: Fix Airtable stipend records still showing incorrect approval status from the ExpenseBot pilot. Use airtable_update_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblStipends00001 to correct at least: (a) rec_stipend_andre_desk, change Approval Status from "Approved" to "Rejected" or "Pending" (the $1,200 standing desk was incorrectly auto-approved above the $800 Mosaic policy limit); (b) rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork, change Approval Status from "Approved" to "Rejected" or "Pending" (the $135 coworking passes were incorrectly auto-approved despite coworking being excluded from Mosaic's policy); (c) recStip000000022, change Approval Status from "Approved" to "Rejected" or "Pending" (Andre Washington's $350 coworking space membership was incorrectly auto-approved despite coworking being excluded from Mosaic's policy — same excluded_categories config bug); (d) recStip000000021, change Approval Status from "Approved" to "Rejected" or "Pending" (Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk was incorrectly auto-approved above the $800 Mosaic policy limit — same string comparison bug as rec_stipend_andre_desk); (e) recStip000000023, change Approval Status from "Approved" to "Rejected" or "Pending" (Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes were incorrectly auto-approved despite coworking being excluded from Mosaic's policy — same excluded_categories config bug as rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork).
OE 15 [Write]: Post a consolidated engineering status breakdown in the #engineering Slack channel (C003) using conversations_add_message. The message should connect all three data integrity failures as a pattern (ExpenseBot config bugs, duplicate receipt fraud, invoice attachment swap), summarize the current state of open tickets and what was found in the investigation, note the Airtable records that were corrected, and explain why tickets are being reprioritized, so the full engineering team has visibility.
OE 16 [Write]: Send an email to Samira Tariq using send_email from dmitri.volkov@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com. The email should propose treating the three validation failures as a single workstream rather than separate backlog items, summarize the pattern across all three incidents, reference the ticket updates and sub-issues created, and include Dmitri's assessment of the scope and dependencies.

✓Run #6The #engineering message explicitly mentions 'ExpenseBot config bugs (ENG-212, 214, 215)' with details about type mismatches: 'max_amount stored as string "800" instead of integer, excluded_categories stored as string instead of array' and 'String comparison 1200 <= "800" evaluated true.'

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #3The final response states 'QuickBooks records were correct: INV-2026-0398 → Sunbelt at $4,800'. The ENG-187 ticket update and the Slack message also reference 'INV-2026-0398 → Sunbelt at $4,800'.

28 The model must search QuickBooks bills with a query or criteria that would return the flagged stipend reimbursement records, using terms related to 'STIP', 'stipend', 'Andre Washington', 'Jordan Ekwueme', 'keyboard', or 'hold' (or similar).
query construction
 ✓✓✓✗✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a `quickbooks_search_bills` call with `fetchAll: true`, which would return all bills including STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097. The final response confirms the holds were found and verified.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows a quickbooks_search_bills call with fetchAll: true which returned all bills including the STIP- stipend bills with admin holds. An earlier attempt used criteria matching STIP (though it errored), and the successful fetchAll returned the stipend records.
✓Run #3The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with criteria matching '%STIP-2026-0094%' and '%STIP-2026-0097%'. Although the tool errored (invalid parameter format), the query construction was targeted at stipend bill records with the correct bill numbers.
✗Run #4The quickbooks_search_bills call used criteria '[['MetaData.LastUpdatedTime', '>', '2026-03-01']]' — a time-based query that is not targeted at stipend or employee-specific records. It also errored and returned no data. No subsequent QuickBooks bills query was made with stipend-specific terms like 'STIP', 'stipend', 'Andre Washington', or 'Jordan Ekwueme'.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows a quickbooks_search_bills call with criteria [['DocNumber', '=', 'STIP-2026-0094']]. While it errored due to API format issues, the agent correctly targeted the stipend bill records using the STIP bill number pattern, demonstrating the correct query intent.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows a quickbooks_search_bills call with {fetchAll: true}, which would return all bills including the flagged stipend reimbursement records STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097.
29 The model must search or list Airtable stipend records with parameters that would return records beyond just the three duplicate-receipt submissions, for example by listing all records in the Stipend Transactions table, or searching by 'Mosaic' or 'Approved' (or similar).
query construction
 ✗✓✗✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The agent searched Airtable by individual employee names ('Kevin Tran', 'Andre Washington', 'Jordan Ekwueme'). While the Andre Washington query was broad enough to return the false approvals (standing desk records), the agent never searched for Kenji Nakamura, whose coworking records (recStip000000023, rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork) are also incorrectly 'Approved'. A query by 'Approved' status or a full table list would have captured all false approvals. The narrow per-name approach missed an entire employee's records.
✓Run #2The agent searched Airtable by 'Andre Washington' which returned all his records including the standing desk ($1,200) and coworking space membership ($350) — records beyond the three known flagged duplicate submissions. This was broad enough to discover the incorrectly approved pilot records.
✗Run #3The agent searched Airtable by specific employee names (Kevin Tran, Andre Washington, Jordan Ekwueme) — only the three known duplicate-receipt suspects. While the Andre Washington search did return the false-approval records (standing desk, coworking), the agent never searched for Kenji Nakamura or used a broad parameter (e.g., filter by 'Approved' status or list all records). Kenji Nakamura's false-approval records were never found, demonstrating the search was not broad enough to discover all incorrectly approved pilot records.
✗Run #4The airtable_search_records calls queried for exactly the three known duplicate-receipt names: Kevin Tran, Andre Washington, and Jordan Ekwueme. No broader search (e.g., by 'Approved' status, 'Mosaic', or listing all records) was performed that would have surfaced Kenji Nakamura's false-approval records from the pilot.
✗Run #5The agent searched Airtable by specific employee names (Kevin Tran, Andre Washington, Jordan Ekwueme) — the three known flagged employees. While the Andre Washington search incidentally returned additional records (standing desk, coworking), the agent never searched broadly (e.g., by 'Approved' status, by 'Mosaic', or listing all records) and completely missed Kenji Nakamura's records (also Mosaic Robotics, incorrectly approved). The query strategy was targeted at known names rather than a broad discovery approach.
✗Run #6The agent searched Airtable only by specific employee names (Kevin Tran, Andre Washington, Jordan Ekwueme), which are the three known duplicate-receipt suspects. While the Andre Washington search did return false-approval records, the agent never searched for Kenji Nakamura or searched broadly by 'Mosaic', 'Approved', or listing all records. This missed all of Kenji Nakamura's false approval records.
